gitCredentials.title: Git Credentials
gitCredentials.description: Stored PATs for cloning private repositories
gitCredentials.info: When you register a project via Git URL (Add Project → Clone from Git URL), select a credential here to clone private repos. Tokens are encrypted at rest and never logged.
gitCredentials.noOrg: No organization configured yet — register a project first.
gitCredentials.orgIdHelp: Your organization's UUID. Find it in any org URL or via the orgs API. Multi-org picker lands with Epic #35.
gitCredentials.name: Name
gitCredentials.tokenHelp: Token is encrypted at rest. After save it cannot be retrieved again — copy elsewhere if you need a backup.
gitCredentials.confirmDelete: Delete this credential? This cannot be undone.

testCredentials.title: Test Credentials
testCredentials.description: Logins your generated tests authenticate with
testCredentials.info: Reference a stored credential from .tfactory.yml (test_credentials + auth: { type: ref }). It is injected only on egress lanes when a subtask sets requires_auth, and scrubbed from every log and artifact. Secrets are encrypted at rest and never returned after creation.
testCredentials.noOrg: No organization configured yet — enter your org ID above.
testCredentials.orgIdHelp: Your organization's UUID. Find it in any org URL or via the orgs API. Multi-org picker lands with Epic #35.
testCredentials.name: Name
testCredentials.secretHelp: Encrypted at rest. After save it cannot be retrieved again — store a backup elsewhere if you need one.
testCredentials.confirmDelete: Delete this credential? This cannot be undone.

apiKeys.title: API Keys
apiKeys.description: Scoped acw_ keys for the stdio MCP control plane. Replaces the host-wide admin token for enterprise deployments.
apiKeys.info: Mint a scoped key, then set $TFACTORY_MCP_KEY (or drop the value in ~/.tfactory/.mcp-key) on the laptop that runs the stdio MCP. The key is shown once at creation — copy it before closing the panel.
apiKeys.orgIdHelp: Your organization's UUID. Multi-org picker lands with Epic #35.
apiKeys.name: Name
apiKeys.confirmRevoke: Revoke this key? Any laptop or shell using it loses access immediately.
apiKeys.copyOnce: Copy this key now — it will not be shown again.
apiKeys.copyOnceHelp: The backend stores only a hash. If you lose the key you have to revoke and mint a new one.

email.noCredentialsConfigured: No OAuth credentials configured. Set Microsoft or Google credentials in the Integrations settings tab.
email.oauthCredentialsDescription: Register an app in Azure AD to enable Outlook email notifications. Required scopes: Mail.Send, User.Read, offline_access.

setup.title: How to register a Microsoft Azure AD app
setup.step1: Go to the <azureLink>Azure App Registrations portal</azureLink>
setup.step2: Click "New registration", name it (e.g. "TFactory Notifications"), select "Accounts in any organizational directory and personal Microsoft accounts", then click Register
setup.step3: From the app overview page, copy the "Application (client) ID" and paste it in the Client ID field below
setup.step4: Go to "Certificates & secrets" → "New client secret" → copy the Value (not the Secret ID) and paste it in the Client Secret field below
setup.step5: Go to "Authentication" → "Add a platform" → "Web" → set the redirect URI to:
setup.step6: Go to "API permissions" → "Add a permission" → "Microsoft Graph" → "Delegated permissions" → add: Mail.Send, User.Read, offline_access
setup.httpsWarning: Azure requires redirect URIs to use HTTPS (except http://localhost). Make sure your deployment uses HTTPS or a reverse proxy that terminates TLS.

email.googleOauthCredentialsDescription: Register an OAuth app in Google Cloud Console to enable Gmail email notifications. Required scopes: gmail.send, userinfo.email.

setupGoogle.title: How to set up Google OAuth
setupGoogle.step1: Go to the <googleLink>Google Cloud Console Credentials page</googleLink>
setupGoogle.step2: Click "Create Credentials" → "OAuth client ID" → select "Web application" as the application type
setupGoogle.step3: Copy the "Client ID" and paste it in the Google Client ID field below
setupGoogle.step4: Copy the "Client secret" and paste it in the Google Client Secret field below
setupGoogle.step6: Go to "Enabled APIs & services" → enable the "Gmail API" for your project

agentProfile.label: Agent Profile
agentProfile.title: Default Agent Profile
agentProfile.sectionDescription: Select a preset configuration for model and thinking level
agentProfile.profilesInfo: Agent profiles provide preset configurations for Claude model and thinking level. When you create a new task, these settings will be used as defaults. You can always override them in the task creation wizard.
agentProfile.phaseConfigurationDescription: Customize model and thinking level for each phase
agentProfile.phaseConfigNote: These settings will be used as defaults when creating new tasks with the Auto profile. You can override them per-task in the task creation wizard.
